entry.title: #01 - Discover the Onboarding Journey with SAP: Your Roadmap to Success
entry.content:
So, you’ve signed the contract with SAP—what’s next? Your onboarding journey has officially begun. 

 

 

 

 

 

Our commitment with this journey is to ensure a smooth and successful implementation of your SAP solution, and it’s divided into four key phases: Get Started with SAP, Plan Your Implementation, Get Enabled, and Prepare to Go Live.

The Onboarding Journey Begins

When the contract is signed, the onboarding process kicks off. Within four days, your designated IT Contact Person (written in the contract) will receive a welcome e-mail from SAP. This e-mail* contains the essential info on how to get started with your SAP solution.

*To ensure that you don't miss our helpful e-mails, the IT Contact should verify the Spam folder.

What’s Inside the Welcome Email?

This welcome email is your first step on the onboarding journey. It directs you to essential assets that will be your companions throughout the implementation:

  • Onboarding Resource Center: This is your one-stop-shop for all things onboarding. It houses a comprehensive collection of guides, checklists, and tools that will help you navigate each phase of your journey.
  • Onboarding Webcast: These webcasts are designed to provide you with deep insights into the onboarding process, offering expert advice and best practices.
  • Embedded Launch Activities (EmLA) Resources: If your solution includes Embedded Launch Activities, these resources will be invaluable. They are tailored to ensure that your launch is as seamless as possible.
  • Preferred Success Information: If you’ve purchased Preferred Success, this email will also provide details on how to access this premium support service, which is designed to accelerate your SAP journey.

Note: It’s important to distinguish this Welcome Email from the Provisioning Email. The Provisioning Email, which you’ll receive later (usually on contract start date), is also sent to the IT Contact but contains critical system information necessary to complete the provisioning process, whereas the Welcome Email focuses on resources to help you get started.

What Should You Do Next?

From the moment the contract is signed until it officially starts, it’s crucial to familiarize yourself with the content of the onboarding journey. Here’s what we recommend:

  1. Explore the Onboarding Resource Center: Take the time to browse through the resources available. This will give you a solid understanding of what to expect and how to prepare for each phase.
  2. Register for the Onboarding Webcast: These sessions are invaluable for getting up to speed with the onboarding process. Make sure to plan, register, and join these sessions to gain insights and clarify any questions you might have.
  3. Incorporate the Four Phases into Your Project Timeline: Use the assets provided to map out the four phases of the onboarding journey within your project timeline. This will ensure that your team is aligned and ready for each stage.
  4. Confirm your IT Contact: Ensure that the IT Contact entered in the SAP contract is the correct person within your organization to receive the Welcome and Provisioning Emails. If you need to update the IT Contact, you can use the User Onboarding Token (if available in your SAP contract), or create a Case to SAP Product Support*.

*The options may vary depending on the SAP Solution.

In Conclusion

The onboarding journey with SAP is meticulously designed to ensure that you’re fully prepared for a successful implementation. By following the steps outlined in the welcome email and leveraging the resources provided, you’ll set a strong foundation for your project. Remember, this journey is not just about getting started—it’s about ensuring long-term success with your SAP solution. So, take the time to explore, plan, and engage with the resources at your disposal. The road to a successful go-live starts here.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series: Customer Onboarding at SAP.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the authors:

  • Rita Cabral Silva is a Customer Onboarding Experience Owner Coordinator and Onboarding Advisor for the SAP IBP solution under Supply Chain Management area. She delivers expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature to successful go-live. Rita has previously worked as a SAP Consultant with seven years of experience, specializing in SAP IBP solutions and leading full-cycle implementation projects
  • Linh Hoang is a strategy and program manager for the customer onboarding at SAP, overlooking the onboarding journey cross-solution areas. With eight years of experience in management and technology consulting, Linh has a deep understanding of customer experience and the critical role of first impressions. Linh is dedicated to ensuring seamless onboarding processes that set the foundation for long-term success.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @RCS1 or @Linh-Hoang.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Unlock Fast Support: Best Channels to report C4C SAP cases
entry.content:
C4C V1 provides a basic IT management system that allows your IT team to manage issues reported by end users directly within the solution. While it offers the convenience of forwarding unresolved issues to SAP support without needing external websites or additional authentication, its capabilities are limited.

How does C4C Built-In Support work?

Within C4C V1, users have access to the "Help Center," where they can navigate through company-specific help documents, SAP product documentation, and other resources like wikis, communities, and product pages. However, the available support tools are role-dependent and somewhat restricted:

 

  • Report Case: End users can report cases but are unable to forward them to SAP. Key users can forward cases directly.

 

  • Process Cases: End users can only check their open cases.

Note: Admin users (key users) must use the admin view under Administrator > Case to process open cases.

  • Support Assistant: it can be used by all users to find solution  but is not linked to the case creation wizard.
  • Expert Chat and Schedule an Expert: These options direct users to the main SAP Support page, requiring S-User authentication.

Forwarding a case to SAP during its creation is only possible for admin users, and the priority must be carefully selected and justified with a valid business impact.

C4C's built-in support allows you to:

  • Support your end users
  • Contact SAP without using an S-User
  • Access internal and external help resources (help articles, documentation, blogs, forums)
  • Access SAPforMe (S-User required)

For complete details on using C4C's built-in support, refer to this link.

Are you only using built-in support to report issues? If so, you're missing out on much more!

C4C V1’s built-in support is a secondary channel for reporting issues, heavily reliant on the tenant's availability. Its integration with SAP support is technically limited to basic features, which is why we strongly recommend using SAPforMe as your primary support channel.

Why choose SAPforMe over C4C V1 built-in support?

Case Creation and Resolution:

  • Channel Selector with built in support assistant : During case creation you’re guided to the most relevant support channel for your situation. 
  •  ISM (Incident Solution Matching): This AI-powered tool suggests solutions at every stage of the case creation process.
  • Support Assistant: Helps you find a solution and create an optimized case f no solution can be offered.
  • Real-Time Channels: When appropriate, the support assistant offers real-time support alternatives to opening a case.
    • Create a Chat: Start a chat from an existing case and communicate directly with the case processor.
    • Schedule an Expert (SaE): Book a 30-minute session with a support engineer from an open case.
    • Escalate a Case: Escalate it to an SAP support manager and schedule a phone conversation.
  • Access to SAP Community: For low-priority or "how-to" questions, the community can provide quick answers.
  • Multiple User Notifications: Notify multiple users when a case is updated.
  • Rate Your Experience: Provide real-time feedback to SAP support 
  • Customer Insight Dashboard: Access a comprehensive overview of your solutions and entitlements.
  • User Access and Maintenance : manage your users and access rights
  • System Landscape and Provisioning
  • And much more.

     

 

SAPforMe offers a vastly superior support experience, making it the preferred choice for managing and resolving issues efficiently. Don't miss out on its advanced capabilities—make SAPforMe your primary support channel today.

For more details and resources about SAPfor Me, please visit our community page

entry.title: How to Create an Effective SAP Support Case: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
How to Create an Effective SAP Support Case: A Comprehensive Guide

“Support us to support You more effectively.”

 

Introduction

In this blog, I will guide you through the steps to create an effective SAP support Case. By following these best practices, you can minimize effort, avoid the infamous “ping-pong” game, and accelerate the resolution process.

As you navigate the process of creating and managing SAP support Cases, it’s essential to leverage all the tools and resources available to you. One valuable resource that SAP offers is the Support Accreditation program, which is available free of charge: Getting Started with Support from SAP (Support Accreditation)

Content of this blog:

  1. What is consulting, what is support
  2. Tests/preparation before Case creation
  3. Common mistakes on reporting side which lead to delayed resolution.
  4. How to Create an Effective SAP Support Case with example
  5. Very High issues
  6. Good to know.

1. What is consulting, what is support

Understanding the distinction between "Support" and "Consulting" in SAP is essential for directing your inquiries to the correct channel. This ensures that your issue is handled appropriately, whether it's a technical problem requiring immediate attention or a request for expert advice on system customization or configuration.

What is Support?

Support is typically concerned with issues related to the standard functionality of SAP software, particularly when something is not working as intended. The following scenarios are considered support cases:

  1. Error/Defect in the Software: This involves situations where a bug or defect in the SAP software prevents it from functioning correctly.
  2. Subsequent Errors Caused by a Software Defect: These are issues that arise as a direct consequence of a known software defect. If an error in the software triggers other problems, this is covered under support.
  3. Problems Implementing Corrections: If you encounter difficulties while applying SAP correction instructions, such as SAP Notes or patches, these are handled by support.
  4. Incorrect Documentation of Standard Functions: Support also addresses issues where the official documentation for standard SAP functions is incorrect or misleading.

What is Consulting?

These services focus on helping you optimize and customize your SAP environment to meet specific business needs. The following scenarios fall under consulting requests:

  1. Configuration and Business Process Analysis: This includes guidance on how to configure the SAP system to align with your specific business processes or requirements. It's about setting up the system, not fixing bugs.
  2. "How-to" Questions on Documented Features: These are inquiries seeking help with understanding or using features that are already documented. They do not involve fixing defects but rather understanding the application of existing functionalities.
  3. Customization and Its Related Issues: If you need help with customizations that are not part of the standard SAP product, or if issues arise due to these customizations, this falls under consulting.
  4. Business Requirements Not Part of the Standard SAP: When your business requires functionalities that are not included in the standard SAP solution, consulting can help develop or implement these custom features.
  5. Modifications: This includes any work related to modifying the core SAP product to meet specific needs, including advice on how these modifications can be implemented. If there is a standard program used for modifications, we can verify the standard program's functionality once a test data is provided for us. Additionally, guidance on how to fill the parameters in our programs is also out of support. 

More information can be found in the SAP Note 83020. 

By understanding and following this distinction, you ensure that your inquiry is handled by the appropriate team, leading to a more efficient and effective resolution process.

Outside the scope of standard SAP Support

When your situation falls outside the scope of standard SAP support, it's important to leverage the appropriate resources to find a solution. Here’s how you can approach this:

1. Use the SAP Search Portal

  • Portal Link: SAP Services & Support Search Portal
  • Action:
    • Search for Solutions: Use the search function to look for SAP Notes, Knowledge Base Articles (KBA), documentation, and other resources that might address your issue.
    • Use Specific Keywords: Enter relevant keywords, such as the specific process, error codes, or functionalities you’re dealing with, to find the most pertinent results. (See SAP Note 2081285)

This ensures you're accessing up-to-date, official information directly from SAP, rather than relying on potentially outdated or incorrect information from general search engines like Google.

2. Involve Your Local Consultant

  • Why Involve Them? Your local consultant has a deep understanding of both your specific business processes and the standard SAP functionality. They can provide tailored advice on how to adapt or modify the system to meet your needs.
  • How They Can Help:
    • Business Process Analysis: They can analyze your specific business requirements and suggest how to configure SAP accordingly.
    • Standard Workarounds: If a direct solution isn’t available, they might suggest standard workarounds or alternative approaches that can achieve the desired outcome.
    • Customization Advice: For issues related to customizations, they can help you understand the impact and suggest adjustments or improvements.

3. Engage with the SAP Community

  • SAP Community Link: SAP Community
  • Purpose: The SAP Community is a forum where SAP users, consultants, and experts share knowledge, solutions, and best practices. It’s an official channel for discussing "how-to" questions and consulting-type inquiries.
  • Action:
    • Post Questions: If you can’t find a solution through the search portal, you can post your question in the community. Be specific about your issue to get the most relevant responses.
    • Search Existing Threads: Often, others have faced similar challenges, and the community might already have threads discussing your issue.
    • Interact with Experts: Engage with SAP consultants, members, and other users who might offer solutions, suggestions, or workarounds.

Additional resources provided by SAP:

  • SAP Help Portal - https://help.sap.com
  • SAP Help Portal Documentation - Support Content | SAP Help Portal
  • Ask an Expert peer- https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/ask-an-expert-peer-is-now-live-in-the-get-support-application-in-sap-for-me/ba-p/13575963

 

2. Tests/preparation before Case creation

1. Check for existing KBA/SAP Note before raising a Case

By doing this, you might find that the issue has already been documented and resolved, saving time and potentially avoiding the need to open a Case at all. (E.g., Services & Support)

2. Share your findings with the Support team, if you create a SAP Case.

3. Test, if the issue is recreatable or one time issue:

  • Reproducible Issues: Providing clear problem description with a detailed step by step instruction with every necessary data how the issue can be recreated.
  • One-Time Issues: Root cause analysis is only possible, if the issue can be recreated, as this allows for a thorough examination of the system logic when the problem occurs. Analyzing an issue from an incorrect or altered state makes it challenging to determine what happened in the past. If the issue is a known problem within standard SAP functionality, SAP may be able to provide a correction report. However, if the issue is custom-related or unknown, your development team will need to create a custom report.

For issues that cannot be recreated, SAP may recommend monitoring the system to identify patterns and common factors, such as noting that the issue frequently occurs between 9-11 AM, potentially pointing to a scheduled job running during that time. This monitoring can help in narrowing down the root cause even when direct recreation isn't possible.

4. Test, if the issue is recreatable in a test system

Log in to the test environment or system where you can safely replicate issues without affecting production data.

5. System connection

Ensure that the connection is opened, and the logon credentials are provided in the Customer Remote Logon Depot. Please test it, before submitting the Case.

6. Test custom modification(s) during the process

Before escalating an issue to SAP, it’s beneficial to involve your development team to verify how the system behaves when only standard SAP programs are called. Understanding whether the problem lies within standard SAP processes (SAP Case) or custom modifications (Consulting>> local Consultant, DEV team needs to be involved) can save time by ensuring that the correct team addresses the issue and can prevent unnecessary delays in the resolution process.

The following blog can be helpful: Troubleshooting to identify modifications during a process

 

3. Common mistakes on reporting side which lead to delayed resolution:

Priority High, Medium, Low

  • Lack of search: A common issue that leads to unnecessary delays is the failure to search for existing solutions or a lack of sufficient knowledge about the system. Before raising a new SAP Case, it's important to thoroughly search the knowledge base, relevant SAP Notes, and available documentation. Often, solutions or workarounds for similar issues already exist. Taking the time to perform a comprehensive search can significantly reduce resolution time and ensure that the problem is handled more efficiently.
  • Unclear issue/steps:
    • Vague Problem Description: If the problem is not described clearly, the support team may struggle to understand the issue, leading to multiple follow-up requests for more information.
    • Missing Steps to Reproduce: Without clear, detailed steps to reproduce the issue, it’s difficult for the support team to diagnose and resolve the problem efficiently.
  • Connection to the system is failed.
    • Case Opened for the Wrong System: Opening a Case for the incorrect system (e.g., wrong system number, installation number, or client) can cause significant delays as the support team may start investigating the issue in the wrong environment. It's crucial to verify and identify the correct system before creating the Case.
    • Reporting Issues for a closed System: If the system is closed, it can prevent the support team from accessing it to diagnose and resolve the issue. Before raising a Case, ensure that the system is available and open for investigation.
    • Missing Logon Credentials: If the Case lacks necessary logon credentials (such as usernames, passwords, client), the support team won't be able to access the system, causing delays.
    • Incorrect Logon Data: Providing incorrect logon details can lead to unnecessary back-and-forth communication to obtain the correct information, delaying the resolution process.
    • Expired/ invalid Logon Data: If the logon credentials provided have expired, the support team will not be able to access the system, which requires additional steps to update or renew the credentials.
    • Restricted Access: If the logon data provided has restricted access that doesn’t allow the support team to fully investigate the issue, this will delay the resolution. Ensure that the credentials provided have adequate access rights to see them.  (See SAP Notes 2361951 and 3195225)
  • Missing authorization: Missing authorizations are a common cause of issues in SAP systems. Before escalating a problem to SAP support, ensure that the user has the necessary authorizations to perform the task in question. Issues related to missing authorizations should be resolved internally through your organization’s security and role management procedures, as these are not typically handled by SAP. Verifying and addressing authorization problems early can prevent unnecessary delays in the resolution process.
  • Issue is one time issue, but it is not mentioned in the problem description.

Priority Very High

Here are additional points that specifically relate to P1 issues:

  • Missing contact information/contact is not reachable : Missing contact information, particularly the reporter’s phone number and email address, is a critical mistake that can lead to delays. Phone number of the reporter is essential for Very high issues to speed up the processing in case of missing information, connection problems. Additionally, it is important to ensure that contact is available 24/7. This accessibility will enable us to respond swiftly and efficiently, minimizing any potential disruptions.
  • Incorrect priority is selected (none justified P1): To avoid over-prioritization, always carefully assess the impact, urgency, and available workarounds for an issue before selecting "Very High" priority. Ensure that only issues that meet the stringent criteria outlined in SAP Note 67739 are given this designation. This approach helps to maintain the efficiency and effectiveness of the support process, ensuring that critical resources are allocated to the most urgent issues.
  • Not consider Workarounds: It's crucial not to overuse the "Very High" priority label. If a workaround exists, it suggests that the immediate threat has been neutralized, and the situation no longer meets the criteria for P1. While the workaround buys time, it’s important to continue working on a permanent fix, but with less urgency, allowing for a more thorough and deliberate resolution process.
  • Consulting inquiries: Consulting inquiries should never be categorized as "Very High" (P1) priority issues. These inquiries generally do not cause immediate harm to business operations or involve system downtime, which are the primary criteria for "Very High" priority classification. Use the correct channel for consulting inquiries. See section ‘Outside the scope of standard SAP Support’.
  • Go live is impacted without official documentation: For P1 issues, especially those claiming critical impacts like an upcoming go-live, official documentation is crucial. It ensures that the reported impact is valid and helps in properly assessing and addressing the issue. Without such documentation, the claim cannot be fully trusted or prioritized as P1. Always request and review documentation before making decisions on priority and resource allocation.

 

4. When you create a Case to SAP, please ensure that the following information is provided:

  • Information related to the system connection:
    • System and Client: Clearly state the system ID(ABC) and the specific client (e.g., 100, 200) where the issue can be analyzed.
    • User Access: If multiple user accounts are provided, specify which user should be used to replicate or analyze the issue.
    • Server Information: If there are multiple servers, indicate which server should be used for analysis.
    • FF User (Firefighter User): If a Firefighter (FF) user account is required for high-privilege access, provide instructions or a guide on how to request and use the FF user. Include details on how to log in, any specific steps needed-
  • Provide the Authorization SAP_ALL to the SAP user to ensure that the analysis will be not interrupted by several missing authorization.
  • Ensure that the R/3 support connection is opened, and the logon credentials are stored in the Customer Remote Logon Deport.
    • Never include or store logon credentials in any discussion or Case description.
    • Before submitting the support Case, test the logon credentials by accessing the system via the support connection.
    • Verify that the logon credentials are not restricted according to the guidelines in SAP Notes 2361951 and 3195225.
  • Issue is recreatable?
    • One time issue
    • Recreatable issue: Detailed problem and step-by-step instruction with every necessary data: Share as much information as you can regarding the issue.
  • Provide screenshots/Attachment:
    Attach screenshots for the Case for better understanding the issue.
  • Provide the reporter’s contact.
    Ensure that the reporter’s contact is updated in the section ‘Case contacts’; phone number with country code, e-mail address. 
  • Result of the test/preparation
    Share your findings with the Support team, if you create a SAP Case.

+1 for High/ Very High issues

Proper business impact description

A well-articulated business impact description is crucial for conveying the urgency and significance of an issue, particularly for Very High priority (P1) cases. Here’s a template and example of how to write a proper business impact description:

  1. A Productive System is Completely Down? (Yes/No)
    1.1 System ID/client:
    2. An Imminent go-live or upgrade is jeopardized? (Yes/No)
    2.1 Planned Go Live Date( with providing official documentation):
    3. The Core Business Processes are seriously affected (Yes/No)
    3.1 What is the Core Business process?
    3.2 How does this impact the process?
    4. Is a workaround available? (Yes/No)
    4.1 if Yes, what is the workaround used?
    5. What is the estimated financial loss within 24hrs
    6. Number of Users affected:
    7. Business Impact Description:
    8. Reporter’s contact and who is available in 24/7: 

Example:

Case Title: Synchronization Failure of Customer and Vendor Data to Business Partner in S/4HANA Migration

Priority: High

Case ID: #345678

Reported By: Maria Gomez, ERP Systems Manager

Contact Information:

  • Phone Number: +1-555-321-0987
  • Email Address: maria.gomez@company.com

Business Impact:

The synchronization failure of Customer and Vendor master data to Business Partner (BP) is causing significant delays in our migration project to S/4HANA. This issue prevents the successful migration of critical master data, impacting the overall project timeline and causing potential data inconsistencies in the new system.

Case is opened for the System:

ABC/123 ( test environment)

Attachments:

[Error_in_MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT.docs]

Problem description:

Hello SAP Support,

We plan to migrate the system to S4HANA for this reason we followed the steps from the CVI Cookbook, but the synchronization is failed in MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT. We are getting the error (copy the short text, message number, long text of the error).

The issue is recreatable in the test environment ABC/123. Example customer for the test system: 12345678.

Reproduction Steps:

  1. Logon to the system ABC/123
  2. Go to Tcode MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT and add the customer 12345678 (see the attached screenshot)
  3. Execute

R/3 support connection is opened, and the logon credentials are updated in the Customer Remote Logon Depot. SAP_ALL authorization is provided for the user.

We have tried to search for the error on the SAP portal, but we did not find any KBA which provide a solution for the above-mentioned error.

No workaround is available.

Thank you and best regards,
Maria Gomez
ERP Systems Manager

 

5. Good to know.

  • Special Cases: There are some special cases such as webservice, Fiori where more information is needed to understand the situation and start the analysis:
    • KBA 3478487-How to create proper webservice Case to SAP
    • KBA 3481621-How to create proper Fiori Case to SAP (BP)
      For other special cases, you could use the search, if the area created a KBA about how to create Cases properly on their area.
  • Speed up the process: Creating duplicate cases, opening multiple chats, or sending repeated Info to SAP support are not effective methods to expedite issue resolution. These actions can lead to confusion, miscommunication, and potential delays in addressing the problem. It’s important to follow the proper escalation procedures and provide all necessary information in the original case to ensure a smooth and efficient resolution process. In case you need help to speed up processing or would like to increase the priority of your case contact the Customer Interaction Center.
    • If the processor is already assigned to the Case: The CIC team will notify the processor for speed up.
    • If the processor has not assigned to the Case yet: The CIC team will notify the whole team for speed up.
      See SAP Note: 560499

  • 1 Case = 1 problem: Avoid reporting multiple questions in the same incident. SAP can best help you, if the communication in the case is regarding one specific topic, which is handled by the experts of that specific area. See SAP Note: 50048, 1038290

  • Problem description does not contain any information, only the attachment: When creating an SAP Case, the system automatically suggests the correct component based on the short and long text descriptions of the problem. Therefore, it is essential to provide comprehensive information in the long text section. An incorrect or incomplete initial component selection can lead to unnecessary delays in processing and resolving the issue.

  • Correction report: SAP can only provide a correction report if the issue is identified as a flaw in the standard software. If the issue arises due to custom modifications or enhancements made to the system, it is the responsibility of the customer to address these problems through custom reports or other internal solutions.

  • System connection cannot be opened due to security reasons: Root cause analysis can only be performed, if the support engineer can recreate the issue and check the system logic using the debugger. If the support engineer is unable to connect to the system, we recommend involving your local development team to debug the issue and share their findings with us. Please note that debugging via a screen-sharing session is not possible.
  • Keep the communication in the Case: To ensure a clear and efficient resolution process, it is crucial to keep all communication within the SAP Case. This centralizes information, making it easier for all parties to follow the issue's history and progress. Avoid using external communication channels like emails or MS teams to discuss the issue, as this can lead to confusion and delays. Consistently updating the Case with relevant details and responses helps ensure that the issue is addressed promptly and accurately.

  • Follow up case: If a follow up Case is created, please refer to the previous Case number and title to identify it. Please also provide the problem description and screenshots for the new case.

  • Modifications: Supporting modifications is out of scope of support. SAP can ONLY support standard programs. To help us assist you better, kindly specify the SAP program you are using and prepare test data for that program.
  • Avoid using ASAP in the SAP Cases: While it's natural to want things resolved "ASAP," we kindly ask that you avoid using phrases like "ASAP" or "Do the needful," as these can create unnecessary pressure. Rest assured; we're always working to help you as quickly as possible.
    To help us better understand and prioritize your needs, please show the urgency of your issue by indicating the priority and filling in the business impact accordingly. This detailed information allows us to assess the situation accurately and ensure that we address your request with the appropriate level of attention and speed. By working together and providing clear details, we can continue to deliver the best service possible.

I hope this blog post was helpful for you. If so, click on "like" or "share". I'm looking forward to your feedback and thoughts or clarification questions you may have in the comment section below.

entry.title: #02 - Access Your Systems: A Key Step in Your SAP Onboarding Journey
entry.content:
Accessing your systems is a crucial part of the onboarding process when implementing an SAP solution. Once the SAP contract is signed, the wheels are set in motion to grant you access to the systems you'll need to manage your SAP environment effectively. Here's what you need to know about accessing your systems and ensuring everything is set up correctly.

Who Gets Access?

When your SAP contract is signed, the customer nominates a Technical Administrator, also referred to as the IT Contact. This person is critical to the onboarding process. They will be assigned as the S-User super administrator, which gives them comprehensive administrative privileges. Additionally, they will receive the all-important provisioning email, which contains the necessary information to access the purchased solution.

Access to the Solution Purchased in the Contract:

Access the main systems or solutions you've acquired from SAP is essential as it will be the foundation to start your implementation. To know the details on how to access to your primary systems, follow the SAP Provisioning guides in the table below (or download the SAP Solutions Provisioning Overview). These guides are also available in the Onboarding Resource Center of each solution and offers detailed instructions on setting up your system.

SAP Cloud ERP

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance

SAP SuccessFactors HCM

  • SAP Sales Performance Management
  • SAP SuccessFactors

SAP Intelligent Spend & Business Network

  • SAP Ariba, SAP Business Network for Procurement and SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration
  • SAP Ariba Category Management
  • SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • SAP Business Network for Asset Management
  • SAP Business Network for Logistics
  • SAP Fieldglass

SAP Supply Chain Management

  • SAP Integrated Business Planning
  • SAP Transportation Management
  • SAP Extended Warehouse Management
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Asset Performance Management
  • SAP Field Service Management
  • SAP Service and Asset Manager
  • SAP Maintenance Assistant
  • SAP Product Lifecycle Management

SAP Customer Experience

  • SAP Commerce Cloud
  • SAP Customer Data Solutions
  • SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement
  • SAP Marketing Cloud
  • SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud
  • SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud v2

SAP Financial Management

  • SAP CPQ
  • SAP Subscription Billing

SAP Training & Adoption

  • SAP Enable Now
  • SAP Learning Hub <100 Licenses
  • SAP Learning Hub >100 Licences

More Solutions

  • SAP Signavio Process Insights
  • SAP LeanIX
  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Access SAP for Me, the single entry point for your entire SAP Porfolio.

SAP for Me is your central access point and the go-to destination to cover all your SAP engagements, allowing you to manage your products, contacts, support engagements, and licenses in a simple and effective way. For some solutions (e.g. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition), accessing SAP for Me (Systems & Provisioning dashboard) is a required step the start the provisioning process and trigger the emails sent to the IT Contact.

To know more details about SAP for Me platform, check this SAP for Me Initial Access Guide. (Remark: Solution intricacies could exist, consult your Account team.) Here you will be able to:

  • Understand What an S-User Is: An S-User is a unique identifier that grants access to SAP systems. The S-User super administrator has the highest level of access and can manage other users’ permissions.
  • Know How to Change the IT Contact: - If the designated IT Contact needs to be changed, it’s essential to know the proper procedure to ensure the new contact is granted the necessary access.
  • Get Access to SAP for Me: To manage your SAP environment effectively, ensure that the IT Contact and other relevant users have access to SAP for Me.
  • Get Access to SAP Learning Hub for SAP Enterprise Support: Access to this learning hub is crucial for enabling your team with the right skills and knowledge. Ensure the appropriate users are enrolled and utilizing these resources.

Recommendations for a Smooth Access Process:

  1. Check for the Provisioning Email: Ensure that your IT Contact checks for the provisioning email as soon as the contract starts. This email is crucial for accessing your systems.
  2. Perform the necessary provisioning tasks: It’s recommended to perform provisioning tasks outlined in the provisioning notification email as close to the contract start date as possible. This will help to avoid any unnecessary issues, such as the user access expiring. Retriggering provisioning takes time and can cause delays in your project.
  3. Create multiple Admin Users: After logging in successfully, create multiple admin users. This step safeguards your system and ensures continuity, even if the project doesn’t start immediately.
  4. First login to SAP for Me: Access SAP for Me to manage your SAP products and get support. This will be your central access point and the go-to destination to cover all your SAP engagements.
  5. Align with your Implementation Partner: Before provisioning any follow-up systems like pre-production and production environments, ensure you’re aligned with your implementation partner. This coordination helps prevent any missteps during the critical setup phases.

In Conclusion

Accessing your systems is a foundational step in your SAP journey. By understanding the roles, following the correct procedures, and utilizing the available resources, you can ensure a smooth and efficient start to your SAP implementation. Make sure your IT Contact is on top of these tasks, and you’ll be well on your way to successfully leveraging your SAP solutions.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the authors:

  • Keith is a system access topic expert in the SAP customer onboarding team and is passionate about customers first successful login to SAP products and support systems.
  • Linh Hoang is a strategy and program manager for the customer onboarding at SAP, overlooking the onboarding journey cross-solution areas.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @KeithB  or @Linh-Hoang.

entry.title: Learn All About How to view or download license keys requested by users in SAP for Me
entry.content:
You can get real-time support anytime and anywhere via Telephone!

In this blog post, you will learn all about How to view and/or download license keys requested by users.

To ensure SAP experience is as smooth and effective (as possible), we suggest (you) Go to 
SAP for Me > License Keys

If your S-user ID has the authorization Request License Keys at the Customer Number level (or CCC level in the case of a Corporate Group), you can view license keys requested by all S-users under your customer number (or CCC Group) in the History of License Key Requests area.

If the authorization is needed see KBA 2891528 - How to use the Self-Service Authorization Request - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

Note: Only requests from the last 3 years are available, based on the current date.

To view the History of License Key Requests, do the following:

  1. Go to SAP for Me > License Keys
  2. Click Display/Hide Main Menu top left (hamburger icon)
  3. Click History of Requests



4. From there you can see My Requests and My Company's Requests with the option to use filters and sort (top right)



5. You can narrow the results between the license keys created by your own S-user ID or expand the search to all license key requests for your company (by all S-users). You can also filter the results based on the following:

  • Installation number
  • Product Version
  • Reason
  • SID
  • Status
  • System No.
  • System Type
  • User  

6. Click on a specific entry to see the most current details of that system (you will not see the original request details).

  • Depending on the product type, the options on the screen will vary (e.g. E-mail License Key, Download License Key, Renew Certificates, etc.)

7. Click Back to return to the History screen.

See Also

  • KBA 2666374 - How to run the Extended Download report showing all System Data and License Keys previously created.

Thanks for reading! 

 

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community 

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: New CQC SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Transition
entry.content:
As part of the Enterprise Support Cloud Edition offering, we’re excited to announce the launch of a new Continuous Quality Check (CQC) SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Transition focused on assisting you in transitioning from SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding 1.0 to SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding. The new service will be live  and available to SAP customers on September 16th. 

The CQC SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Transition is designed to provide a comprehensive evaluation of the transition process from Onboarding 1.0 to Onboarding. 

Benefits of this service:

  • The benefits of this service include:
  • Review current settings, use of features, and enablement of key functions.
  • Analyze effort required for the transition.
  • Receive a detailed service report that highlights effort required and key areas for consideration not only for current enabled features but also for not enabled features and features only available in new Onboarding version.
  • Optimize the possibility for a smooth transition journey.

Please check the CQC SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Transition infosheet in SAP Support Portal Page to learn about the key features and benefits of this service, when to use it (or not) and further details of the delivery.

How to request the service:

Remote Services can be requested via the Get Support application in SAP for Me (SAP Note 1296527). When submitting your request, please select:

  • Product: “Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services”
  • Product function: “Service request for a Cloud Solution”

If you need assistance in submitting your case, you may contact your local Customer Interaction Center (CIC).

Further Support and Empowerment via SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Value Maps are a support and empowerment program from SAP Enterprise Support and are available to you via the SAP Learning Hub. A one-time sign in with your S User is required, and everything thereafter is free as part of your entitlements. We provide:

  • Prescriptive Guidance
  • Empowerment
  • Social Collaboration
  • Expert Access

Check out the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page  for more and start engaging today!

entry.title: #03 - Setting Clear Objectives: The Foundational Digital Transformation
entry.content:
When embarking on an SAP implementation project, one of the most critical steps is to set clear objectives. These objectives will not only guide your project but also help you measure its success once completed. Here’s how to establish those objectives effectively and ensure that everyone involved is aligned with the project’s goals.

Why Clear Objectives Matter

Setting clear objectives is essential because it defines what you want to achieve with your SAP implementation. These objectives provide direction and purpose, allowing your team to focus on what truly matters. Without clear objectives, projects can drift off course, leading to wasted time, resources, and ultimately, a lack of tangible results.

Moreover, having a well-defined set of objectives allows you to establish success criteria. By knowing what success looks like from the outset, you can create a measurement plan to track progress and evaluate the project's outcomes against the initial goals.

Aligning Stakeholders with Project Expectations

One of the most common pitfalls in any project is misalignment among stakeholders. It’s crucial that all stakeholders understand the expectations of the project from the beginning. This includes not only the technical aspects of the implementation but also the broader business transformation goals.

Introducing SAP Value Management Framework

The help you identify key value drivers, measure their impact with precise KPIs, and optimize through continuous improvement.

 

The SAP Value Lifecycle Manager (VLM) is a key accelerator used by SAP for value management service delivery. This tool is publicly accessible and provides a range of valuable features for both customers and partners Benchmarking Surveys, Business Case Development and Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Analysis. The VLM tool enables you to evaluate your processes and key performance indicators (KPIs), helping you discover the value of the Intelligent Enterprise. It’s powered by best-in-class value content and insights, making it an invaluable resource for setting clear, actionable objectives.

VLM will soon also be covering the value of AI to further improve business process excellence.

Conclusion: A Mindset for Success

The mindset you bring to your SAP implementation project is crucial. By defining a clear North Star and operational objectives, you will get all stakeholders aligned and significantly increase the odds that your Digital and AI transformation will deliver high business value within time and budget. Remember, this is not just a technical upgrade—it's an opportunity to transform your entire business. Keep your goals clear, your team focused, and your eyes on the long-term value that SAP can deliver.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

Koert Breebaart is a Business Transformation and Customer Experience professional and expert in developing and driving digital business transformations. He is author of a business book on Customer Centricity. Koert works with SAP Switzerland as Head of Value Advisory. Previous to SAP Koert worked with SingTel and Accenture. He is recognized for his thought leadership in the areas of value engineering, customer experience, sales excellence, project management and global operating models.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @koertbreebaart or @Linh-Hoang.

entry.title: (Re)Discover the Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators from SAP with our new Elearning
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

Did you know that SAP’s Out-of-the-Box Business Key Figures, now referred to as Process Performance Indicators (PPIs), have been available for over 15 years? Since their introduction, these PPIs have become fundamental to a wide range of process performance analysis tools for SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA. They played a critical role in tools such as the SAP Solution Manager-based SAP Business Process Improvement for SAP Solutions and Business Process Monitoring, and the SAP Innovation and Optimization tools. Now, it is also one of the key pillars of SAP Signavio Process Insights.

We are happy to share that we released a new e-learning course designed to guide you through the capabilities of SAP’s PPIs and the tools available within your SAP ES maintenance that will allow you to harness their potential to maximize your business’s efficiency.

Here’s what you can expect from the course: 

  1. Get an overview of SAP’s PPIs. Learn what Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators are, and understand how they provide immediate insights into business processes.
  2. Discover the powerful process analysis tools that come as part of your existing SAP maintenance contract and how you can leverage them to continuously optimize business processes.
  3. Learn how you can easily get started with the tools and apply the PPIs to your specific business process improvement goals.

Access the E-Learning:

Leveraging the Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators to Improve Business Processes

Requirement: Registration for the SAP Learning Hub is required to utilize e-learning sessions.  If you are not currently registered, please do so here: SAP Enterprise Support Academy

Join us in the Value Maps: 

If you are not yet signed up for Value Maps, please join via this link.

You will discover new opportunities to understand and analyze the bottlenecks in business processes and improve the identified challenges. The Business Process Transformation value map helps you to innovate your business processes in an ever-changing world. The SAP ES Value Maps require no additional costs to access for our SAP Enterprise Support and SAP PSLE customers.

Please don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "SAP Signavio | Business Process Transformation" topic).

entry.title: Join Us at ASUG Tech Connect 2024: Elevate Your SAP Journey with Exclusive Sessions
entry.content:
ASUG Tech Connect 2024 is just around the corner, offering an exciting opportunity to explore the latest SAP innovations, connect with industry leaders, and dive deep into sessions that will empower your SAP transformation. With insightful presentations and expert-led discussions, you'll gain practical insights to drive success in your organization. 

We are thrilled to announce a series of must-attend sessions hosted by our top executives and experts. Whether you're focusing on AI, cloud strategies, or system operations, these sessions will provide the knowledge and tools you need to optimize your SAP experience. Plus, don't miss the chance to visit our booth within the SAP area, which will be dedicated to SAP CloudALM and Customer Support.

Let’s help you transform your business.

Here’s what you can expect:

November 12, 2024 at 10:15 AM

Crafting an AI Roadmap: Strategies for Measuring AI Benefits
Speakers: Stefan Steinle and Wieland Schreiner
Join Stefan and Wieland as they delve into the intricacies of creating a robust AI roadmap tailored to your organization's needs. This session will cover essential strategies for planning and implementing AI initiatives while ensuring measurable benefits. Learn how to assess the impact of AI on your operations, optimize your processes, and quantify the value added, enabling a clear path to AI-driven success.

November 12, 2024 at 10:15 AM

Choosing the Right Path to SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Speaker: Jörg Marenk
Discover the various options SAP offers for transitioning to the cloud, including SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Private Edition, and two-tier scenarios. Explore which is the best fit for your organization and gain insights into the latest version, SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.

November 12, 2024 at 11:15 AM

Support Expert Session: Harnessing AI and Automation for an Enhanced Support Experience
Speakers: Mohammed Ajouz, Oliver Huschke
Explore how to leverage AI and intelligent automation to maximize your support experience. In this session, discover how to use live support channels and preventative services to receive exceptional value and improve support outcomes. Learn about the latest AI enhancements, including Incident Solution Matching, automatic translation, and channel recommender available through SAP for Me and Built-in Support. Gain practical insights and tips from our experts to help you access the right support services more effectively. Leave with actionable strategies to make the most of your support interactions and achieve better results.

November 12, 2024 at 11:15 AM

Control SAP BTP Changes with SAP Cloud ALM: Best Practices and Observability
Speakers: Umesh Jagadesh, Evan Stoddard
Unlock the full potential of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) with SAP Cloud ALM. Discover how to effectively manage and control changes in SAP BTP to drive efficiency and innovation. Learn best practices for SAP BTP operations, including the use of Terraform for automatic resource management, central monitoring with SAP Cloud ALM, and new administrative features for usage and cost monitoring. Explore how SAP Cloud ALM serves as the central observability platform for custom CAP applications, using OpenTelemetry for comprehensive monitoring across local and central tools.

November 12, 2024 at 2:25 PM

Transform Your SAP Experience: Unlocking the Power of Cloud and Support
Speakers: Geoff Scott, Stefan Steinle
Ready to revolutionize your SAP journey? Join Stefan and Geoff as they reveal how harmonizing cloud operations with customer support leads to seamless implementations. Discover the game-changing potential of Cloud ALM and RISE with SAP. Learn from real-world success stories and empower your organization to thrive in the cloud.

November 13, 2024 at 10:15 AM

SAP Runs SAP: Leading the AI Revolution in Customer Support
Speakers: Stefan Steinle, Jens Trotzky
Discover our AI-driven customer support model, where innovative strategies fuse AI with customer service to enhance efficiency and satisfaction. Explore our journey as we set new standards in AI-powered service excellence. Leverage this blueprint to seamlessly integrate AI into your organization and unlock success through the SAP for Me portal.

November 13, 2024 at 11:05 AM

Revolutionizing Operations: SAP Cloud ALM Best Practices for UiPath’s SAP S/4HANA Journey
Speakers: Sanjoy Rath, Jorge Ruiz
In the digital era, efficient process monitoring is crucial. UiPath excels by integrating with third-party products like Office365, MS Teams, Slack Channel, and other SaaS Products for real-time alerts and collaborative problem-solving.  Attend this session to hear how the integration of SAP Cloud ALM enhances UiPath's capabilities by providing comprehensive monitoring for RISE with SAP systems, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, and boundary systems.  

November 13, 2024 at 11:15 AM

How to Move from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
Speakers: Evan Stoddard, Vijay Prakash
Transitioning from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM is crucial for enhancing implementations, operations, and service. This session will guide you through various adoption scenarios and provide actionable insights for planning and executing your transition. Learn about the tools and content available now, as well as what's on the product roadmap, to streamline your move to SAP Cloud ALM.

 

November 13, 2024 at 2:40 PM

Transform Your Business with an Integrated Transformation Toolchain
Speaker: Jörg Marenk
Discover how to navigate a clean core ERP transformation using an integrated business transformation toolchain with the RISE with SAP methodology. Learn how to effectively transform your business processes and application landscape while maintaining a lean environment. Explore how to utilize SAP LeanIX, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP Signavio solutions to drive your strategy from planning to execution, ensuring a clean core and long-term sustainability.

November 14, 2024 at 12:45 PM

Adopt Clean Core During your Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Speakers: Wieland Schreiner, Jörg Marenk, Evan Stoddard
Discover how SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Readiness Check can enhance your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system conversion by offering robust planning, execution, and monitoring capabilities. Learn how to adopt a clean core ERP for a smooth transition.

November 14, 2024 at 4:15 PM

SAP for Me: Bundles Unleashed
Speakers: Anna Withum, Carsten Schwarz
The new "Bundles" feature empowers you to align your SAP systems seamlessly with your business processes. This innovative tool allows you to use your own terminology, ensuring you fully grasp the impact of any disruptions and clearly identify which processes are affected. Don't miss this opportunity to learn how "Bundles" can enhance your operational efficiency and provide you with greater clarity and control. Join us to explore this feature in depth and discover how it can benefit your organization.

Make sure to add these sessions to your personal agenda and visit us at our booth in the SAP area, where we’ll be showcasing SAP CloudALM and discussing how our customer support solutions can transform your SAP journey.
